commits: bd3943c: docs(0.7.0): draft ADR for vector binary quantization (Pack 1+2)
findings_addressed: Step 0 of dev/plans/prompts/0.7.0-PERF-VECTOR-QUANT-HANDOFF.md — drafted dev/adr/ADR-0.7.0-vector-binary-quant.md with all seven mandated sections (Problem, Decision, Alternatives, Consequences, Architectural-lever accounting, Open questions/HITL gates, Status/next actions) plus frontmatter (title, date 2026-05-27, target_release 0.7.0, desc, blast_radius with file:line touch sites for Pack 1 and Pack 2, status: draft, HITL-required).

Cited AC-013 canonical-CI measurement (p50=2048ms, p99=2327ms) from dev/plans/runs/0.7.0-PERF-EXP-W4.1-ac013-canonical-output.json against the proposed 80/300 ms envelope, with root-cause analysis (3 GB scan / ~1.5 GB/s memory-read ceiling).
Recorded the five rejected alternatives with mechanism + reason + research-doc citation: vectorlite, sqlite-vec ANN alpha, Rust-side usearch/instant-distance, embedder swap, partitioning-alone.

Reaffirmed PCACHE2 as the 0.7.0 architectural lever (per ADR-0.7.0-ac020-architectural-lever.md) and classified binary-quant + rerank as a data-encoding change; documented the Pack 1-only fallback if a reviewer counts it as a second lever.

Documented HITL-locked decisions: recall floor 0.90 @ k=10, source_type partition_key (cardinality ~6), sqlite-vec pin stays =0.1.7, embedder unchanged, single-writer + projection-cursor contracts preserved.

Enumerated open questions / HITL gates: numeric AC-013/AC-019 budgets (post-P2-CANONICAL), K-value selection if recall<0.90 at K=64, vec0 16-column metadata budget, vec_quantize_binary normalization expectations.
agent_verify_result: skipped (doc-only slice; no code paths exercised)
next_step_for_orchestrator: spawn codex reviewer on the ADR draft
